evidence: `routines/runtime_seams.go` and `docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go` both define large `runtime` structs composed only of function fields and constructors that mostly rebind one function per op with `seams.RequireSeam*` wrappers.
`lifecycle` and `network` each declare their own `dockerClientNew` plus `SetClientFactory` seam, duplicating the same dependency override abstraction for the same boundary.

`broadcast/collectors/collectors.go` composes configuration, orchestration, click, and system concerns in one function chain (`ConstructPierInfo` -> `runtimeSnapshotForPiers` -> `collectUrbitViewInputs`) with no abstraction boundary for data providers.
This indicates repeated cross-cutting indirection layers and a single layer doing orchestration duties across modules instead of clean composition.

findings.summary: Runtime seam layers are duplicated across packages with mostly pass-through behavior, increasing indirection and cognitive load.
evidence: `routines/runtime_seams.go` defines `dockerRoutineRuntime` and `dockerRoutineStateOps`/`dockerRoutineContainerOps` wrappers where most fields are direct passthroughs to module-level functions.
`docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go` defines a parallel seam graph (`dockerRuntime`, `dockerRuntimeContextOps`, `dockerRuntimeContainerOps`, etc.) with equivalent pass-through initialization.
Both files create largely parallel abstractions for similar boundaries without evidence of policy separation beyond seam injection/testing indirection.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared seam-building utility or unified runtime abstraction layer so orchestration and routine call-sites share a small set of explicit constructor arguments instead of duplicated per-package op-struct scaffolding.

findings.summary: Docker client factory seams are repeated in multiple layers with inconsistent surface area, causing duplicate test/DI setup and fragmented ownership.
evidence: `goseg/docker/network/network.go` exports `dockerClientNew` and `SetClientFactory` as a mutable seam to override `dockerclient.New`.
`goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go` repeats the same pattern (`dockerClientNew` + `SetClientFactory`) instead of reusing a single shared client-seam contract.
`goseg/dockerclient/client.go` already owns the version-aware client construction and ping-caching behavior, so the upper layers are re-wrapping a behavior that is already centralized.
findings.suggestion: Move client creation seams behind one package-level abstraction (or explicit runtime parameter) and remove duplicated mutable factory globals from both callers so all docker client injection is configured consistently.

findings.summary: Collector logic aggregates state acquisition, policy, and view assembly, making it hard to test and reuse as a true abstraction.
evidence: `ConstructPierInfo` in `collectors` not only transforms state but also calls into `config` (`StartramSettingsSnapshot`, `GetStartramConfig`, `LoadUrbitConfig`) and orchestration (`runtimeSnapshotCollector` via `GetShipStatus`) directly.
`collectUrbitViewInputs` loads config (`UrbitConf`), pulls container status info, resolves network/minIO state, and performs derived view mapping in a single function.
This conflates provider boundaries: callers cannot inject isolated data providers without rebuilding broad global seams, so abstraction does not provide clear replacement points.
findings.suggestion: Split collectors into explicit provider interfaces (state providers, network/status providers, and view-mapping composer) and inject these at runtime so each stage has single responsibility and test seams are focused.
